title: Full Deal Cycle Simulation
description: Simulate an entire B2B sales cycle from initial prospecting through to a signed contract. This capstone project ties together everything you've learned: research, outreach, discovery, demo, proposal, negotiation, and close.
whyItMatters: Real sales isn't a series of isolated skills — it's a continuous flow from one stage to the next, where each interaction builds on the last. This simulation forces you to connect the dots: your prospect research informs your outreach, your outreach earns a discovery call, your discovery shapes your demo, your demo feeds your proposal, and your proposal frames the negotiation. Completing this project gives you a portfolio-ready case study and the confidence that you can run a deal end-to-end.
skillsLearned: Time and priority management across multiple deal stages

steps.title: Prospect and Research (2 hours)
steps.description: Select a target company and build a complete account intelligence profile. Identify 3-4 target contacts across the buying committee. Research the company's challenges, technology stack, competitive landscape, and recent news. Create your outreach strategy.
steps.tips: Choose a company complex enough to have a buying committee (50+ employees) but not so large that the deal feels abstract. A mid-market SaaS or tech company is ideal.

steps.title: Outbound Outreach (3 hours)
steps.description: Write and 'send' a complete multi-channel outreach sequence: 5 emails, 2 LinkedIn touches, and 2 cold call scripts targeting different stakeholders. Document the buyer's 'responses' (role-play or simulate) and how you handled them. At least one contact should agree to a discovery call.
steps.tips: Simulate realistic responses: one person ignores you, one person says 'not interested', one person asks for more info, and one agrees to a call. Document how you'd handle each scenario.

steps.title: Discovery Call (3 hours)
steps.description: Run a full discovery call simulation. Document the call: agenda, questions asked, buyer responses, pain points uncovered, qualification criteria (MEDDIC scorecard), and agreed next steps. The discovery should uncover at least 2 quantified pain points and identify the economic buyer.
steps.tips: The discovery should surface something unexpected — a pain point you didn't anticipate or a competitor you didn't know about. Real deals always have surprises.

steps.title: Demo and Presentation (4 hours)
steps.description: Build and deliver a tailored demo using Tell-Show-Tell, anchored to the pain points from discovery. Include a slide deck, demo script, and preparation for 5 tough questions. Document the buyer's reactions and feedback. The demo should result in a request for a proposal.
steps.tips: Include at least 3 attendees in the demo scenario with different priorities. Show how you'd address each stakeholder's concerns while keeping the demo focused and on time.

steps.title: Proposal and Business Case (4 hours)
steps.description: Write a complete proposal with executive summary, problem statement, proposed solution, ROI model, implementation plan, pricing (with 2-3 options), and next steps. The proposal should be professional enough to send to a real buyer.
steps.tips: Use the buyer's own words and numbers from discovery. 'Based on the $300K in annual churn you mentioned...' is far more compelling than generic industry stats.

steps.title: Negotiation and Close (4 hours)
steps.description: Simulate the negotiation phase. The buyer pushes back on price, asks for a shorter contract, wants additional scope for free, and mentions a competitor's lower quote. Document your negotiation strategy, the back-and-forth, the final agreed terms, and the close. Write the closing email and internal deal summary.
steps.tips: The negotiation should include at least one moment where you need to hold firm and one moment where you make a strategic trade. Document your reasoning for each decision.

deliverables: Account intelligence profile and outreach strategy
Complete multi-channel outreach sequence with simulated responses
Discovery call transcript and completed MEDDIC scorecard
Demo slide deck, script, and tough questions preparation
Full client proposal with ROI model
Negotiation strategy document and outcome summary
Closing email and internal deal summary
A portfolio-ready deal case study (2-3 pages) summarising the entire journey

rubric.description: Each stage builds logically on the previous one. Insights from discovery appear in the demo. Demo feedback shapes the proposal. The story is consistent throughout.
All deliverables are at a professional standard — ready to share with a hiring manager as portfolio pieces. Documents are well-written, well-formatted, and error-free.
The simulation demonstrates strategic decision-making: who to target, what to prioritise in discovery, how to structure the demo, when to trade vs hold firm in negotiation.
The deal case study includes honest reflection on what went well, what could be improved, and what the candidate learned about their own selling approach.
